name: Torpedo Alley
description: Three Akula-class submarines are threading the Denmark Strait in column. Close the trap.
briefing:
CLASSIFICATION: SECRET

TO: Commander, Strike Group WHISKEY
FROM: COMSUBNORLANT INTEL WATCH
DTG: 121400Z OCT 1985

Three contacts. Akula-class. Transiting the Denmark Strait in column formation. Running shallow, running fast. Probably repositioning to support North Sea operations.

The strait is 180 miles wide but the safe lane is 40. Biologics are dense. Bottom bounce is everywhere. Detection ranges are degraded by at least forty percent.

USS Bremerton (SSN-698) is already in the water ahead of them, holding position below the thermal layer. She knows they're coming. They don't know she's there.

USS Spruance (DD-963) is racing south from the Greenland coast to cut off retreat. She will not arrive clean.

This will be close. It will be ugly. The strait is narrow enough that someone is going to take a torpedo in the first ten minutes.

Do not let it be you.

Rules of engagement: weapons free on confirmed hostile subsurface contacts.
